heading: Review of applications
label: 11825 Review of applications
notes: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in subsec. (b)(1)(A), was in the original “this part” and was translated as reading “this chapter” to reflect the probable intent of Congress because subtitle B of title III of , which comprises subchapters I to III of this chapter, does not contain parts.Pub. L. 100–690

children.text: the relative cost and effectiveness of the proposed project or activity in carrying out purposes for which the requested grant or contract is authorized to be made,
the extent to which such project or activity will incorporate new or innovative techniques,

the increase in capacity of the State or the public or nonprofit private agency, organization, institution, or individual involved to provide services to address the illicit use of drugs by runaway and homeless youth,
the extent to which such project or activity serves communities which have high rates of illicit drug use by juveniles (including runaway and homeless youth),
the extent to which such project or activity will provide services in geographical areas where similar services are unavailable or in short supply, and
the extent to which such project or activity will increase the level of services, or coordinate other services, in the community available to eligible youth.

children.label: A
children.text: announcing the availability of funds to carry out this subchapter,

children.label: B
children.text: stating the general criteria applicable to the selection of applicants to receive such funds, and

children.label: C
children.text: describing the procedures applicable to submitting and reviewing applications for such funds.

As part of such process, each application referred to in subsection (a) shall be subject to peer review by individuals (excluding officers and employees of the Department of Justice and the Department of Health and Human Services) who have expertise in the subject matter related to the project or activity proposed in such application.

children.heading: Expedited review
children.label: c Expedited review
children.text: The appropriate Federal officer shall expedite the consideration of an application referred to in subsection (a) if the applicant demonstrates, to the satisfaction of the such officer, that the failure to expedite such consideration would prevent the effective implementation of the project or activity set forth in such application.1So in original. The word “the” probably should not appear.1
